questions.stem: Under ASC 842, which of the following criteria would classify a lease as a finance lease for the lessee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The lease term is for 50% of the remaining economic life of the asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: The present value of lease payments equals 85% of the fair value of the asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ownership of the asset transfers to the lessee at the end of the lease term

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lease contains a renewal option

questions.explanation: ASC 842-10-25-2 provides five criteria for finance lease classification. Transfer of ownership at the end of the lease term is one of them. The other criteria include: a purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised, lease term is for the major part (typically 75%) of the remaining economic life, present value of payments amounts to substantially all (typically 90%) of the fair value, and the asset is specialized with no alternative use.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンスリースの分類基準は5つある。リース期間終了時の所有権移転はその1つである。その他: 行使が合理的に確実な購入オプション、リース期間が残存経済的耐用年数の大部分(通常75%)、支払いのPVが公正価値のほぼ全て(通常90%)、資産が特殊で代替的用途がないこと。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 5-year operating lease with annual payments of $10,000 due at the end of each year. The lessee's incremental borrowing rate is 6%. The present value of the lease payments is $42,124. What is the initial measurement of the right-of-use asset?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $42,124 plus any initial direct costs and prepaid lease payments

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, the right-of-use asset is initially measured at the amount of the lease liability ($42,124), plus any lease payments made at or before commencement, plus any initial direct costs incurred by the lessee, minus any lease incentives received.
questions.explanationJa: 使用権資産(ROU)の当初測定額 = リース負債($42,124) + 開始日以前に支払ったリース料 + 当初直接コスト - 受領したリースインセンティブ。したがって、$42,124に前払リース料と当初直接コストを加算した金額となる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, how is the lease expense for an operating lease recognized by the lessee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest expense on the lease liability and amortization of the right-of-use asset separately

choices.label: B
choices.text: A single lease cost on a straight-line basis over the lease term

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only when lease payments are made

choices.label: D
choices.text: Front-loaded, with higher expense in earlier periods

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-25-6 states that for an operating lease, a lessee shall recognize a single lease cost, calculated so that the cost is allocated over the lease term on a generally straight-line basis. This differs from a finance lease, where amortization and interest are recognized separately, resulting in front-loaded expense.
questions.explanationJa: オペレーティングリースでは、借手はリース期間にわたり定額配分された単一のリース費用を認識する。ファイナンスリースでは償却費と利息を別々に認識し費用が前倒しになるのに対し、オペレーティングリースは定額(straight-line)である。

questions.stem: Lessee has an existing operating lease with 3 years remaining and a carrying value of the right-of-use asset of $30,000 and lease liability of $32,000. The lease is modified to extend the term by 2 years with no other changes. The modification does not grant an additional right of use. How should the lessee account for this modification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a new separate lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remeasure the lease liability and adjust the right-of-use asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a gain or loss for the difference

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue to account for the original lease without adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-8, if a lease modification does not result in a separate lease, the lessee remeasures the lease liability at the modification date using a revised discount rate, and adjusts the right-of-use asset by the same amount as the change in the lease liability.
questions.explanationJa: リースの変更が独立したリースとならない場合、借手は変更日に改訂された割引率でリース負債を再測定し、リース負債の変動額と同額で使用権資産を調整する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, what is the maximum lease term (at commencement) for a lessee to elect the short-term lease exemption?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 6 months

choices.label: B
choices.text: 12 months

choices.label: C
choices.text: 12 months and the lease does not include a purchase option that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise

choices.label: D
choices.text: 24 months

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-25-2 permits a lessee to elect not to apply the recognition requirements for short-term leases. A short-term lease is defined as a lease that, at the commencement date, has a lease term of 12 months or less and does not include an option to purchase the underlying asset that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise.
questions.explanationJa: 短期リースについては認識要件を適用しない選択が可能である。短期リースとは、開始日においてリース期間が12か月以下であり、かつ借手が行使することが合理的に確実な購入オプションを含まないリースをいう。

questions.stem: Entity F sells a building to Entity G for its fair value of $1,000,000 (carrying value $700,000) and simultaneously leases it back. The leaseback is classified as an operating lease and does not transfer control back to Entity F. What gain should Entity F recognize at the transaction date?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The portion of the gain related to the rights transferred to Entity G

choices.label: D
choices.text: $300,000 amortized over the lease term

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-40-25-3, if the transfer of the asset qualifies as a sale, the seller-lessee recognizes only the amount of any gain or loss that relates to the rights transferred to the buyer-lessor. The portion relating to the right-of-use asset retained through the leaseback is not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 資産の移転が売却として適格な場合、売手兼借手は買手兼貸手に移転した権利に関連する利益・損失のみを認識する。リースバックを通じて保持する使用権資産に関連する部分は認識しない。

questions.stem: A lease agreement requires the lessee to pay $5,000 per month plus 2% of monthly sales exceeding $100,000. Under ASC 842, how should the variable payments based on sales be treated in measuring the lease liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included in the lease liability using an estimate of future sales

choices.label: B
choices.text: Excluded from the lease liability and recognized in the period incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included using the expected value method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalized separately from the lease liability

questions.explanation: ASC 842-10-30-5 states that variable lease payments that depend on an index or rate are included in the lease liability, but variable payments based on performance or usage (such as sales-based payments) are excluded from the measurement of the lease liability and recognized in profit or loss in the period in which the obligation is incurred.
questions.explanationJa: 指数またはレートに連動する変動リース料はリース負債に含まれる。一方、業績または使用量に基づく変動支払い(売上ベースの支払いなど)はリース負債の測定から除外し、義務が発生した期間に損益で認識する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, a lessor classifies a lease as a sales-type lease. At commencement, the fair value of the asset is $500,000, the carrying amount is $400,000, and the present value of lease payments is $500,000. What is the lessor's gross profit at commencement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-1, at commencement of a sales-type lease, the lessor derecognizes the underlying asset, recognizes a net investment in the lease, and recognizes any selling profit or loss. The gross profit equals the fair value (or PV of lease payments if lower) minus the carrying amount: $500,000 - $400,000 = $100,000.
questions.explanationJa: 販売型リースの開始時に、貸手は原資産の認識を中止し、リースの正味投資を認識し、売却損益を計上する。粗利益 = 公正価値(またはリース料PVが低い場合はその額) - 帳簿価額 = $500,000 - $400,000 = $100,000。

questions.stem: When determining the present value of lease payments, what discount rate should a lessee use if the rate implicit in the lease is not readily determinable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The risk-free rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: The lessee's incremental borrowing rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: The prime rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lessor's implicit rate

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-30-3 states that a lessee should use the rate implicit in the lease unless that rate is not readily determinable, in which case the lessee uses its incremental borrowing rate. The incremental borrowing rate is the rate the lessee would have to pay to borrow on a collateralized basis over a similar term.
questions.explanationJa: 借手はリースの計算利子率(rate implicit in the lease)を使用する。その利率が容易に算定できない場合は追加借入利子率(incremental borrowing rate)を使用する。追加借入利子率とは、借手が類似の期間にわたり担保付で借入を行う際に支払う利率である。

questions.stem: A lessee enters a finance lease with an initial right-of-use asset of $100,000 and a lease term of 10 years. The asset has no residual value. Using straight-line amortization, what is the annual amortization expense for the right-of-use asset?

choices.label: D
choices.text: It varies each year based on the effective interest method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-7, for a finance lease, the right-of-use asset is amortized on a straight-line basis (unless another systematic basis is more representative) from the commencement date to the earlier of the end of the useful life or the end of the lease term. $100,000 / 10 years = $10,000 per year.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンスリースの使用権資産は、開始日から耐用年数の終了またはリース期間の終了のいずれか早い方まで定額法で償却する(他の規則的な基準がより適切な場合を除く)。$100,000 / 10年 = 年間$10,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Inc. enters a 5-year finance lease. The initial right-of-use asset and lease liability are both measured at $500,000. The implicit rate is 6%. The ROU asset is amortized on a straight-line basis over the lease term. What is the total lease-related expense recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: For a finance lease, total expense = Amortization of ROU asset + Interest on lease liability. Amortization = $500,000 / 5 years = $100,000. Interest = $500,000 × 6% = $30,000. Total Year 1 expense = $100,000 + $30,000 = $130,000.

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Alpha Corp. enters a 5-year operating lease with the following annual payments due at year-end: Year 1 $40,000; Year 2 $45,000; Year 3 $50,000; Year 4 $55,000; Year 5 $60,000. What is the lease expense Alpha should recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-25-6, operating lease expense is recognized on a straight-line basis regardless of the payment pattern. Total payments = $40,000 + $45,000 + $50,000 + $55,000 + $60,000 = $250,000. Annual straight-line expense = $250,000 / 5 = $50,000.

questions.stem: Beta Corp. enters a finance lease on January 1, Year 1, with an initial lease liability of $350,000. The annual lease payment is $90,000, due at the end of each year. The rate implicit in the lease is 8%. What is the lease liability balance at December 31, Year 1 (after the first payment)?
questions.explanation: Year 1 interest on lease liability = $350,000 × 8% = $28,000. Payment of $90,000 is split: $28,000 interest and $62,000 principal reduction. Lease liability at December 31, Year 1 = $350,000 - $62,000 = $288,000. Equivalently: $350,000 + $28,000 - $90,000 = $288,000.

questions.stem: Gamma Corp. has an operating lease with 3 years remaining. The current lease liability is $150,000 and the ROU asset is $140,000. The lease is modified (not a separate lease), and the present value of the remaining payments under the modified terms, using a revised discount rate, is $195,000. What is the ROU asset balance immediately after the modification?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-4, when a lease is modified and does not qualify as a separate lease, the lessee remeasures the lease liability to $195,000. The change in liability = $195,000 - $150,000 = $45,000 increase. The ROU asset is adjusted by the same amount: $140,000 + $45,000 = $185,000.
questions.explanationJa: リースの変更が独立したリースとして適格でない場合、借手はリース負債を再測定する。リース負債 = $195,000。負債の変動 = $195,000 - $150,000 = $45,000増加。使用権資産も同額で調整: $140,000 + $45,000 = $185,000。

questions.stem: Seller Corp. sells a building (carrying value $700,000) to Buyer Corp. at its fair value of $1,000,000, and simultaneously leases it back under an operating lease. The present value of the leaseback payments is $250,000. How much gain does Seller Corp. recognize at the transaction date?
questions.explanation: Total gain = $1,000,000 - $700,000 = $300,000. Under ASC 842-40-25-3, the gain recognized is limited to the proportion of rights transferred. Rights retained (ROU proportion) = PV of leaseback payments / FV = $250,000 / $1,000,000 = 25%. Gain deferred (related to retained rights) = 25% × $300,000 = $75,000. Gain recognized = $300,000 - $75,000 = $225,000.

questions.stem: Delta Corp. enters a 6-year operating lease. The present value of lease payments (lease liability) is $420,000. Before commencement, Delta paid a $30,000 lease payment and incurred $12,000 in initial direct costs. Delta also received a $22,000 lease incentive from the lessor. What is the initial measurement of the right-of-use asset?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, ROU asset = Lease liability + Payments made before commencement + Initial direct costs - Lease incentives received. ROU = $420,000 + $30,000 + $12,000 - $22,000 = $440,000.
questions.explanationJa: 使用権資産 = リース負債 + 開始前に支払ったリース料 + 当初直接コスト - 受領したリースインセンティブ。ROU = $420,000 + $30,000 + $12,000 - $22,000 = $440,000。

questions.stem: A lessor enters a sales-type lease on January 1, Year 1. The underlying equipment has a carrying amount of $450,000 and a fair value of $600,000. The present value of all lease payments equals $600,000. The implicit rate is 7%. What total income does the lessor recognize in Year 1 (selling profit plus interest income)?
questions.explanation: At commencement: Selling profit = FV - CV = $600,000 - $450,000 = $150,000. The lessor recognizes a net investment in the lease of $600,000. Year 1 interest income = $600,000 × 7% = $42,000. Total Year 1 income = $150,000 + $42,000 = $192,000.

questions.stem: Epsilon Corp. enters a finance lease. The present value of the annual lease payments, discounted at the implicit rate, is $280,000. The lessee guarantees a residual value of which the present value is $45,000. The lessee also incurs $8,000 of initial direct costs. What is the initial lease liability?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-1, the lease liability includes the PV of lease payments, which encompasses fixed payments and guaranteed residual values. Lease liability = PV of annual payments + PV of guaranteed residual = $280,000 + $45,000 = $325,000. Initial direct costs ($8,000) are added to the ROU asset, not the lease liability. ROU = $325,000 + $8,000 = $333,000.
questions.explanationJa: リース負債にはリース料のPVが含まれ、固定支払いおよび残価保証が対象となる。リース負債 = 年間支払いのPV + 残価保証のPV = $280,000 + $45,000 = $325,000。当初直接コスト($8,000)はリース負債ではなく使用権資産に加算する。ROU = $325,000 + $8,000 = $333,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Zeta Corp. enters a finance lease with a lease liability of $500,000. Annual lease payments are $150,000, due at the end of each year. The implicit rate is 10%. What is the interest expense on the lease liability for Year 2?

Eta Corp. has a finance lease ROU asset with a carrying value of $300,000 at the beginning of Year 3 (after cumulative amortization). Due to market changes, Eta performs an impairment test per ASC 360. The undiscounted future cash flows from sublease are $250,000, and the fair value of the ROU asset is $220,000. What impairment loss should Eta recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-9, ROU assets are tested for impairment under ASC 360 (long-lived assets). Step 1 (recoverability test): Carrying value ($300,000) > Undiscounted cash flows ($250,000), so the asset is impaired. Step 2 (measurement): Impairment loss = Carrying value - Fair value = $300,000 - $220,000 = $80,000.
questions.explanationJa: 使用権資産はASC 360(長期性資産)に従い減損テストを行う。ステップ1(回収可能性テスト): 帳簿価額($300,000) > 割引前キャッシュフロー($250,000) → 減損あり。ステップ2(測定): 減損損失 = 帳簿価額 - 公正価値 = $300,000 - $220,000 = $80,000。

questions.stem: Lessee A enters into a contract for office space that includes a lease component (rent: $10,000/month) and a non-lease component (maintenance services). The standalone price of maintenance is $2,000/month. Lessee A does NOT elect the practical expedient to combine components. How should Lessee A allocate the total payments?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allocate the entire payment to the lease component

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allocate based on relative standalone prices: $10,000 to lease, $2,000 to maintenance

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allocate 50% to each component

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocate the entire payment to the non-lease component

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-33, a lessee that does not elect the practical expedient must allocate the consideration in the contract to lease and non-lease components on a relative standalone price basis. The lease component ($10,000) and non-lease component ($2,000) are allocated based on their observable or estimated standalone prices.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-33により、実務上の便法を選択しないレッシーは、契約の対価をリース要素と非リース要素に独立価格の比率で配分しなければならない。リース要素（$10,000）と非リース要素（$2,000）はそれぞれの独立価格に基づき配分する。

questions.stem: Company B is a lessee under a 10-year master lease for office space. Company B subleases the space to Company C for 6 years. Company B should classify the sublease based on which underlying asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The underlying asset (the office space itself)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The right-of-use asset arising from the master lease

choices.label: C
choices.text: The sublease payments receivable

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fair value of the master lease

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-1A, an intermediate lessor (sublessor) classifies a sublease by reference to the right-of-use asset arising from the head lease, not the underlying asset itself. This is because the sublessor does not own the underlying asset but has a right-of-use asset from the head lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-1Aにより、転貸人（中間レッサー）は転貸リースを原資産ではなく、ヘッドリースから生じる使用権資産を基準に分類する。転貸人は原資産を所有しておらず、ヘッドリースからの使用権資産を有しているためである。

questions.stem: Lessor D offers Lessee E a $50,000 cash incentive to sign a 5-year operating lease with annual payments of $120,000. How should Lessee E account for the lease incentive?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the $50,000 as other income in Year 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce the right-of-use asset by the $50,000 and recognize reduced lease expense over the lease term

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record the $50,000 as a deferred liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Offset the $50,000 against the first year's lease payment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, lease incentives received by the lessee reduce the right-of-use asset. The ROU asset is initially measured as the lease liability plus initial direct costs, plus prepaid lease payments, minus lease incentives received. The reduced ROU asset results in lower amortization expense over the lease term.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5により、レッシーが受け取るリース・インセンティブは使用権資産を減額する。使用権資産はリース負債＋初期直接コスト＋前払リース料−受取リース・インセンティブで当初測定する。減額された使用権資産により、リース期間にわたる償却費が減少する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, when a lessee is involved in the construction of an asset before the lease commencement date and the arrangement is NOT considered to provide the lessee control of the asset during construction, how should the lessee account for payments made during the construction period?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize the payments as construction in progress

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the payments as prepaid rent or deposits

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense the payments as incurred

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the payments as a right-of-use asset immediately

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842, if the lessee does not control the asset during construction, the payments are treated as prepaid rent or deposits. The lessee does not recognize the asset or liability until the lease commencement date. This differs from prior guidance under ASC 840 where build-to-suit accounting could result in the lessee being deemed the owner during construction.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842では、レッシーが建設中に資産を支配していない場合、支払いは前払賃料または預託金として処理する。リース開始日まで資産や負債を認識しない。これはASC 840の旧基準で建設中にレッシーが所有者とみなされ得た処理とは異なる。

questions.stem: Lessee F enters into a 5-year lease with two 3-year renewal options. Lessee F has made significant leasehold improvements that are expected to have value through the first renewal period. It is reasonably certain that Lessee F will exercise the first renewal option but not the second. What is the lease term?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 5 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: 8 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: 11 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: 6 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-26, the lease term includes the noncancelable period plus periods covered by renewal options that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise. Significant leasehold improvements that create an economic incentive to renew support the conclusion that exercise is reasonably certain. Lease term = 5 + 3 = 8 years.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-26により、リース期間は解約不能期間に、レッシーが行使することが合理的に確実な更新オプション期間を加えたものである。重要な賃借権改良は更新の経済的インセンティブを創出し、行使の合理的確実性を裏付ける。リース期間 = 5 + 3 = 8年。

questions.stem: Lessor G enters into a direct financing lease. The fair value of the leased equipment is $500,000, the cost is $450,000, and initial direct costs are $10,000. The net investment in the lease equals the present value of lease payments plus the unguaranteed residual value. What selling profit, if any, does Lessor G recognize at lease commencement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 selling profit recognized at commencement

choices.label: B
choices.text: $40,000 selling profit recognized at commencement

choices.label: C
choices.text: No selling profit at commencement; it is deferred and recognized over the lease term

choices.label: D
choices.text: $50,000 recognized in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-7, in a direct financing lease, the lessor defers any selling profit at commencement. The net investment is measured such that the selling profit is embedded in the carrying amount of the lease and recognized over the lease term as part of the interest income. Initial direct costs also reduce the yield.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-7により、直接金融リースではレッサーは開始時に販売益を繰り延べる。正味投資額は販売益がリースの帳簿価額に組み込まれるよう測定され、リース期間にわたり利息収益の一部として認識される。初期直接コストも利回りを減少させる。

questions.stem: Lessor H enters into a 5-year operating lease for equipment with a cost of $400,000 and an estimated useful life of 10 years. Annual lease payments are $60,000. How should Lessor H recognize lease income and depreciation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Lease income of $60,000 per year; depreciation of $40,000 per year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Lease income of $60,000 per year; no depreciation during the lease term

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lease income of $48,000 per year; depreciation of $40,000 per year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lease income of $60,000 per year; depreciation of $80,000 per year

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-11, for an operating lease, the lessor continues to recognize the underlying asset and depreciates it in accordance with ASC 360. The lessor recognizes lease income on a straight-line basis over the lease term. Annual lease income = $60,000. Annual depreciation = $400,000 / 10 years = $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-11により、オペレーティング・リースではレッサーは原資産の認識を継続し、ASC 360に従い減価償却する。リース収益はリース期間にわたり定額で認識。年間リース収益 = $60,000。年間減価償却 = $400,000 / 10年 = $40,000。

questions.stem: A parent company leases office space to its wholly owned subsidiary. Under ASC 842, how should related-party leases be classified and measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Based on the legally enforceable terms and conditions of the lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: At fair market value regardless of the stated lease terms

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a capital contribution from parent to subsidiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: Related-party leases are excluded from ASC 842

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-12, related-party leases are classified and accounted for based on the legally enforceable terms and conditions of the lease, even if those terms differ from what would be negotiated between unrelated parties. The financial statements should disclose the related-party nature of the lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-12により、関連当事者リースは法的に強制力のあるリースの条件に基づき分類・会計処理する。無関係な当事者間で交渉される条件と異なる場合でも同様である。財務諸表ではリースの関連当事者性を開示する。

questions.stem: Lessee J has a 10-year finance lease. In Year 4, Lessee J terminates the lease early and pays a termination penalty of $80,000. The remaining right-of-use asset carrying amount is $200,000 and the lease liability is $230,000. What gain or loss should Lessee J recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $50,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $280,000

questions.explanation: Upon early termination, the lessee derecognizes the ROU asset and lease liability and records the termination penalty. The gain/loss = Lease liability removed ($230,000) − ROU asset removed ($200,000) − Termination penalty paid ($80,000) = $230,000 − $200,000 − $80,000 = −$50,000 (loss of $50,000).
questions.explanationJa: 早期終了時、レッシーは使用権資産とリース負債の認識を中止し、終了ペナルティを計上する。損益 = 消滅するリース負債($230,000) − 消滅する使用権資産($200,000) − 終了ペナルティ($80,000) = $230,000 − $200,000 − $80,000 = −$50,000（損失$50,000）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, which of the following events would trigger a remeasurement of the lease liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in the lessee's credit rating

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change in the assessment of whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise a renewal option

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in the general interest rate environment

choices.label: D
choices.text: A decline in the fair value of the leased asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-4, a lessee must remeasure the lease liability when there is a change in the lease term due to a reassessment of whether it is reasonably certain to exercise (or not exercise) a renewal option, purchase option, or termination option. Changes in credit ratings, general interest rates, or asset fair values do not trigger remeasurement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-35-4により、更新オプション、購入オプション、または終了オプションの行使の合理的確実性の再評価によりリース期間が変更された場合、レッシーはリース負債を再測定しなければならない。信用格付け、一般金利、資産の公正価値の変動は再測定のトリガーとならない。

questions.stem: Lessee K enters into a 5-year finance lease for equipment with annual payments of $20,000. Lessee K guarantees a residual value of $15,000 at the end of the lease. The expected residual value is $10,000. What amount of the residual value guarantee should be included in the lease liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $15,000 (present value of full guaranteed amount)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $10,000 (present value of expected residual)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $5,000 (present value of expected deficiency)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because the guarantee is contingent

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5(c), the lessee includes in the lease liability amounts probable of being owed under residual value guarantees. For a lessee, the full guaranteed residual value ($15,000) is included in the measurement of the lease liability, not just the expected deficiency.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5(c)により、レッシーは残存価値保証に基づき支払う可能性が高い金額をリース負債に含める。レッシーの場合、予想される不足額のみではなく、保証残存価値の全額（$15,000）をリース負債の測定に含める。

questions.stem: Lessee L enters into a 10-month lease for office equipment with no renewal option and no purchase option. The monthly payment is $3,000. Lessee L elects the short-term lease exemption. How should Lessee L account for this lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a right-of-use asset and lease liability at commencement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize lease expense on a straight-line basis over the lease term without recording a ROU asset or lease liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalize the total lease payments as an asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record as a finance lease with amortization and interest

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-25-2, a short-term lease (12 months or less at commencement, with no purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised) may be recognized on a straight-line basis over the lease term without recording a ROU asset or lease liability. The 10-month lease with no options qualifies.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-2により、短期リース（開始日時点で12ヶ月以下、行使が合理的に確実な購入オプションなし）は、使用権資産やリース負債を計上せず、リース期間にわたり定額でリース費用を認識できる。10ヶ月でオプションなしのリースは要件を満たす。

questions.stem: A company enters into a single lease for land and a building. The land element has an indefinite life. Under ASC 842, how should the land and building components be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both are classified together as a single lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: The land and building are separate lease components, and each is classified independently

choices.label: C
choices.text: The land is always an operating lease; the building follows standard classification criteria

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both must be classified as finance leases

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-28, land and building are generally considered separate lease components because the lessee can benefit from each independently. Each component is classified separately based on the ASC 842 classification criteria. The land component, with its indefinite life, typically fails the economic life and purchase option tests.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-28により、土地と建物はレッシーがそれぞれ独立して便益を得られるため、通常は別個のリース要素とみなす。各要素はASC 842の分類基準に基づき個別に分類する。耐用年数が無限の土地要素は、通常、経済的耐用年数や購入オプションの基準を満たさない。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee M enters into a 5-year finance lease. The initial right-of-use asset is $500,000 and the lease liability is $500,000. In Year 1, Lessee M records amortization of $100,000 and interest expense of $30,000, and makes lease payments of $115,000. What are the ROU asset and lease liability balances at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ROU asset: $400,000; Lease liability: $415,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: ROU asset: $400,000; Lease liability: $385,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: ROU asset: $385,000; Lease liability: $415,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: ROU asset: $430,000; Lease liability: $415,000

questions.explanation: ROU asset: $500,000 − $100,000 amortization = $400,000. Lease liability: $500,000 + $30,000 interest − $115,000 payment = $415,000. In a finance lease, the ROU asset is amortized separately and the liability is increased by interest and decreased by payments, similar to a financed purchase.
questions.explanationJa: 使用権資産：$500,000 − 償却$100,000 = $400,000。リース負債：$500,000 + 利息$30,000 − 支払$115,000 = $415,000。ファイナンスリースでは、使用権資産は別途償却し、負債は利息で増加・支払で減少する（割賦購入と同様）。

questions.stem: Lessor N enters into a 5-year operating lease with the following annual rent schedule: Year 1: $0 (rent-free period), Years 2-5: $100,000 per year. What annual lease income should Lessor N recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $100,000 in Years 2 through 5; $0 in Year 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: $80,000 per year for 5 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: $100,000 per year for 4 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: $50,000 per year for 5 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-11, operating lease income is recognized on a straight-line basis over the lease term unless another systematic basis is more representative. Total rent = $0 + $100,000 × 4 = $400,000. Straight-line annual income = $400,000 / 5 = $80,000 per year.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-11により、オペレーティング・リース収益は、他の体系的な基準がより適切でない限り、リース期間にわたり定額で認識する。賃料合計 = $0 + $100,000 × 4 = $400,000。定額年間収益 = $400,000 / 5 = $80,000/年。

questions.stem: Lessee P enters into a lease with annual payments of $50,000 adjusted for CPI changes. At commencement, the CPI is 200. At remeasurement, the CPI is 210. The original lease liability was based on $50,000 payments. What payment amount should be used for the remeasured lease liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 (original amount)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $52,500 (adjusted for CPI increase)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The variable portion is excluded from lease liability

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-40, variable lease payments that depend on an index or rate are included in the lease liability using the index or rate at commencement. When a remeasurement event occurs, the payments are updated using the current index. Remeasured payment = $50,000 × (210 / 200) = $52,500.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-40により、指数または料率に連動する変動リース料は、開始日時点の指数・料率を使用してリース負債に含める。再測定事象が発生した場合、現在の指数を使用して支払額を更新する。再測定後の支払額 = $50,000 × (210 / 200) = $52,500。

questions.stem: Lessee Q has an operating lease with an initial ROU asset of $300,000 and a lease term of 5 years. There are no lease incentives, prepayments, or impairment. After Year 2, what is the carrying amount of the ROU asset?

choices.label: D
choices.text: It depends on the lease liability balance

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-7, for an operating lease, the ROU asset is subsequently measured at the lease liability balance, plus any prepaid/initial direct costs, minus any lease incentives and cumulative amortization. With straight-line lease expense and no other adjustments, the ROU asset amortizes evenly. After Year 2: $300,000 − ($300,000 / 5 × 2) = $300,000 − $120,000 = $180,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-35-7により、オペレーティング・リースの使用権資産は、リース負債残高に前払・初期直接コストを加算し、リース・インセンティブと累積償却額を控除して事後測定する。定額リース費用で他の調整がない場合、使用権資産は均等に償却される。第2年末: $300,000 − ($300,000 / 5 × 2) = $180,000。

questions.stem: Lessee R incurs the following costs in negotiating and arranging a lease: legal fees for lease negotiation ($5,000), commission paid to a broker ($8,000), and costs of employee time spent reviewing the lease ($3,000). Which costs should be included in the initial measurement of the right-of-use asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $16,000 (all three costs)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $13,000 (legal fees and broker commission only)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $5,000 (legal fees only)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $8,000 (broker commission only)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-30-9, initial direct costs are incremental costs that would not have been incurred if the lease had not been obtained. Legal fees ($5,000) and broker commissions ($8,000) are incremental. Internal employee costs ($3,000) would have been incurred regardless and are not initial direct costs. Total = $5,000 + $8,000 = $13,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-30-9により、初期直接コストはリースを取得しなければ発生しなかった増分コストである。法務費用（$5,000）と仲介手数料（$8,000）は増分コスト。内部従業員コスト（$3,000）はリースの有無にかかわらず発生するため初期直接コストではない。合計 = $5,000 + $8,000 = $13,000。

questions.stem: Lessee S adopted ASC 842 using the modified retrospective transition method with the optional transition practical expedient (effective date as the date of initial application). Which of the following statements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Comparative periods must be restated under ASC 842

choices.label: B
choices.text: A cumulative-effect adjustment is recognized in retained earnings at the beginning of the period of adoption

choices.label: C
choices.text: All existing leases must be reassessed for classification under ASC 842

choices.label: D
choices.text: Short-term leases at transition must be capitalized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-65-1(c), entities using the modified retrospective method with the practical expedient recognize a cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings at the beginning of the period of adoption. Comparative periods continue to be presented under ASC 840. The package of practical expedients allows entities to not reassess lease classification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-65-1(c)により、実務上の便法を使用した修正遡及法を適用する企業は、適用開始期間の期首に利益剰余金の累積的影響額の調整を認識する。比較期間はASC 840に基づき表示を継続。一括の実務上の便法により、リース分類の再評価は不要となる。

questions.stem: Lessor T has an existing leveraged lease under ASC 840 at the time of ASC 842 adoption. Under the transition guidance, how should Lessor T account for the leveraged lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify the leveraged lease as a direct financing lease under ASC 842

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continue to apply the leveraged lease model under ASC 840 until the lease expires or is modified

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immediately derecognize the leveraged lease and record a new lease under ASC 842

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify the leveraged lease as an operating lease under ASC 842

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-65-1(cc), leveraged leases that commenced before the effective date of ASC 842 are grandfathered. Lessors continue to apply the leveraged lease accounting model from ASC 840 until the lease expires, is modified, or the lessor otherwise derecognizes it. New leveraged leases are not permitted under ASC 842.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-65-1(cc)により、ASC 842の発効日前に開始したレバレッジド・リースは既存のまま適用が継続される。レッサーはリースが満了、変更、またはその他の理由で認識中止されるまでASC 840のレバレッジド・リース会計モデルを適用し続ける。ASC 842では新たなレバレッジド・リースは認められない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, which of the following criteria, if met, would classify a lease as a finance lease for the lessee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The lease term is for 60% of the remaining economic life of the underlying asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: The present value of lease payments equals 85% of the fair value of the underlying asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: The lease transfers ownership of the underlying asset to the lessee by the end of the lease term

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lease contains an option to extend that is reasonably certain to be exercised

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-2, a lessee classifies a lease as a finance lease if ANY of the following criteria is met: (1) ownership transfers by end of lease, (2) purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised, (3) lease term is for major part (generally 75%+) of remaining economic life, (4) PV of payments amounts to substantially all (generally 90%+) of fair value, or (5) the asset is specialized with no alternative use. Option A (60%) and B (85%) do not meet the thresholds. Option D (extension option) is not itself a classification criterion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-2により、ファイナンスリースの分類基準：(1)所有権移転、(2)購入オプション行使がほぼ確実、(3)リース期間が経済的耐用年数の大部分（75%以上）、(4)支払現在価値が公正価値のほぼ全て（90%以上）、(5)特殊資産。60%や85%は基準未達。

questions.stem: Lessee Corp. enters a 5-year operating lease with annual payments of $100,000 due at the end of each year. The lessee's incremental borrowing rate is 6%. What is the initial right-of-use asset and lease liability (rounded)? (PV of annuity at 6%, 5 years = 4.2124)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $421,236 asset and $500,000 liability

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-1, the lease liability is measured at the present value of lease payments not yet paid, discounted at the rate implicit in the lease (or the lessee's incremental borrowing rate if not readily determinable). PV = $100,000 × 4.2124 = $421,236. Under ASC 842-20-30-5, the ROU asset is initially measured at the amount of the lease liability, adjusted for prepayments, lease incentives, and initial direct costs. With no adjustments, the ROU asset equals the liability: $421,236.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-1により、リース負債は未払リース料の現在価値。$100,000 × 4.2124 = $421,236。ASC 842-20-30-5により、使用権資産は初期測定でリース負債と同額（調整項目なしの場合）。両方$421,236。

questions.stem: Lessee A signs a 10-year operating lease. The lessor provides a $50,000 tenant improvement allowance (TIA) payable at lease commencement. How does the TIA affect the lessee's initial measurement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reduces the initial ROU asset by $50,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized as deferred revenue and amortized over 10 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognized as a reduction of rent expense in the first year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increases the initial ROU asset by $50,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5(c), lease incentives received by the lessee reduce the measurement of the ROU asset. When the lessor pays a TIA at commencement, the lessee records: debit Cash $50,000, credit Lease Liability $0 (no change if already reflected in payments), and the ROU asset is reduced by the $50,000 incentive. The lower ROU asset results in lower amortization expense over the lease term.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5(c)により、リースインセンティブは使用権資産の測定を減額する。TIA$50,000を受領すると、使用権資産が$50,000減少する。これにより、リース期間にわたる償却費用が低くなる。

questions.stem: Lessor Corp. (a manufacturer) leases equipment with a fair value of $500,000 and a carrying amount of $350,000. The lease qualifies as a sales-type lease. Annual lease payments are $120,000 for 5 years at an implicit rate of 6% (PV factor = 4.2124). What profit does the lessor recognize at lease commencement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-1, at commencement of a sales-type lease, the lessor derecognizes the underlying asset, recognizes a net investment in the lease, and recognizes selling profit or loss. The net investment = PV of lease payments = $120,000 × 4.2124 = $505,488. Selling profit = net investment (capped at fair value for revenue) minus carrying amount. Revenue = fair value = $500,000. Cost of goods sold = $350,000. Selling profit = $500,000 − $350,000 = $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-1により、セールス型リース開始時に売上利益を認識。収益 = 公正価値$500,000（純投資額ではなく）。売上原価 = $350,000。売上利益 = $150,000。

questions.stem: Company B contracts with a data center provider for dedicated server space. The contract specifies a particular floor and rack location that only Company B can use. Company B directs how and for what purpose the servers are used. Does this arrangement contain a lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because there is an identified asset and Company B has the right to control its use

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because it is a service contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only if the contract term exceeds 12 months

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because the data center provider maintains the servers

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-2, a contract contains a lease if it conveys the right to control the use of an identified asset for a period of time in exchange for consideration. An identified asset exists (specific floor and rack), and Company B has the right to direct the use. Under ASC 842-10-15-3, the right to direct use exists when the customer directs how and for what purpose the asset is used. The supplier's maintenance does not negate the lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-2により、特定された資産の使用を支配する権利を移転する契約はリースを含む。特定のフロアとラックが識別され、Company Bが使用方法と目的を指示できるため、リースに該当する。供給者のメンテナンスはリースを否定しない。

questions.stem: Seller-Lessee Corp. sells a building to Buyer-Lessor Corp. and simultaneously leases it back. The leaseback is for 95% of the building's remaining economic life. Under ASC 842, how should this transaction be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a sale and finance leaseback, with gain recognition

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a failed sale — a financing transaction for both parties

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a sale and operating leaseback with deferred gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a sale with immediate full gain recognition

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-40-25-1, for a sale-leaseback to qualify as a sale, the transfer must qualify as a sale under ASC 606. Additionally, ASC 842-40-25-3 states that if the leaseback would be classified as a finance lease, the transfer does not qualify as a sale. Since the leaseback covers 95% of the economic life, it meets the finance lease criterion. Therefore, the transaction is accounted for as a financing: the seller-lessee keeps the asset and records a financial liability; the buyer-lessor records a financial receivable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-40-25-3により、リースバックがファイナンスリースに分類される場合、資産の移転は売却として認められない。リースバックが経済的耐用年数の95%をカバーするためファイナンスリース基準を満たし、金融取引として処理する。

questions.stem: Lessee C signs a lease for office space that includes both the use of the space (lease component) and common area maintenance (non-lease component). Total annual payment is $150,000. The SSP of the lease component is $120,000 and CAM is $40,000. If Lessee C does NOT elect the practical expedient, how much is allocated to the lease component?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-33, if the practical expedient is not elected, the lessee allocates consideration based on relative standalone prices. Total SSP = $120,000 + $40,000 = $160,000. Lease component allocation = ($120,000 / $160,000) × $150,000 = $112,500. If the practical expedient were elected, the entire $150,000 would be treated as the lease component.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-33により、実務上の便法を選択しない場合、相対的独立価格で配分する。リース要素 = ($120,000 ÷ $160,000) × $150,000 = $112,500。便法を選択すれば$150,000全額がリース要素。

questions.subtopic: Finance Lease - Interest and Amortization Pattern
questions.stem: How does the total periodic expense pattern differ between a finance lease and an operating lease for a lessee under ASC 842?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Finance lease has higher expense in early years (front-loaded); operating lease has straight-line expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both have straight-line expense patterns

choices.label: C
choices.text: Operating lease has higher expense in early years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Finance lease has lower expense in early years (back-loaded)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842, a finance lease recognizes interest expense on the lease liability (which decreases over time) separately from amortization of the ROU asset (typically straight-line). Total expense = interest + amortization is front-loaded because interest is higher in early periods. An operating lease recognizes a single lease cost on a straight-line basis over the lease term (ASC 842-20-25-6), resulting in level expense each period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842では、ファイナンスリースは利息費用（減少）と償却費（定額）を別々に認識し、合計費用は初期に偏る（前倒し型）。オペレーティングリースはASC 842-20-25-6により、リース期間にわたり定額でリース費用を認識する。

questions.stem: Lessee D has a lease for 10,000 square feet. After 3 years, the lease is modified to reduce the space to 6,000 square feet (a 40% reduction) with a proportional decrease in rent. How should the partial termination be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease the ROU asset and lease liability proportionally (40%), and recognize any difference as a gain or loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remeasure the entire lease as a new lease

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment until the original lease term ends

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write off the entire ROU asset and record a new one

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-25-8(a), a modification that decreases the scope of a lease (partial termination) requires the lessee to decrease the carrying amount of the ROU asset proportionally (based on the reduction in use — here 40%). The lease liability is remeasured using a revised discount rate. Any difference between the proportional decrease in the ROU asset and the decrease in the lease liability is recognized as a gain or loss in the period of modification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-8(a)により、リース範囲を縮小する変更（一部解約）では、使用権資産を使用減少に比例して減額（40%）。リース負債は新割引率で再測定。使用権資産の減額とリース負債の減額の差額は損益として認識。

questions.stem: Lessee E has a 10-year lease with annual payments of $50,000, adjusted annually for changes in the Consumer Price Index (CPI). At commencement, CPI is 250. At the beginning of Year 3, CPI increases to 260. What annual payment is used to remeasure the lease liability at the beginning of Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 (no remeasurement for index changes)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $50,000 remeasured only at lease commencement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-5, when variable lease payments depend on an index or rate, the lease liability is remeasured when there is a change in the payments resulting from a change in the index or rate. The new payment = $50,000 × (260/250) = $52,000. The lease liability is remeasured using the remaining 8 years of payments at $52,000, discounted at the original (or revised if applicable) discount rate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-35-5により、指数に連動する変動リース料は、指数の変動による支払変更時にリース負債を再測定する。新支払額 = $50,000 × (260/250) = $52,000。残り8年間の$52,000を割引率で再測定。

questions.subtopic: Lessor - Direct Financing Lease Initial Measurement
questions.stem: Lessor F leases equipment with a carrying amount of $400,000 and fair value of $400,000. Annual lease payments are $95,000 for 5 years. The unguaranteed residual value is estimated at $30,000. The implicit rate is 7%. Under a direct financing lease, what is the initial net investment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PV of lease payments plus PV of unguaranteed residual value

choices.label: B
choices.text: PV of lease payments only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fair value of the asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Carrying amount of the asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-30-1, the lessor's net investment in a direct financing lease is the sum of: (1) the lease receivable (PV of lease payments) and (2) the unguaranteed residual asset (PV of the unguaranteed residual value). In a direct financing lease, no selling profit is recognized at commencement because the fair value equals the carrying amount (or any profit is deferred). Revenue is recognized as interest income over the lease term.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-30-1により、ダイレクト・ファイナンシング・リースの純投資 = リース債権（リース料のPV）+ 無保証残存価値資産（無保証残存のPV）。ダイレクト・ファイナンシングでは開始時に売上利益を認識しない。

questions.stem: Lessee G signs a 7-year lease for equipment with an option to purchase the equipment at the end of the lease term for $5,000. The estimated fair value at that time is $50,000. What effect does this purchase option have?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The purchase option is reasonably certain to be exercised and is included in the lease payments

choices.label: B
choices.text: The purchase option is excluded because it is not mandatory

choices.label: C
choices.text: The purchase option is included only if the lessee formally commits

choices.label: D
choices.text: The purchase option has no effect on lease classification

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-26, a purchase option is considered reasonably certain to be exercised when there is a significant economic incentive to do so. An option to purchase for $5,000 when the expected fair value is $50,000 represents a bargain purchase option. The exercise is reasonably certain, so the $5,000 is included in the lease payments for measurement purposes and the lease is classified as a finance lease (transfer of ownership criterion).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-26により、購入オプションは行使に重要な経済的インセンティブがある場合に行使がほぼ確実とみなされる。$5,000で$50,000相当の資産を購入できるバーゲン購入オプションは行使確実。$5,000をリース料に含め、ファイナンスリースに分類。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, how should a lessee test the right-of-use asset for impairment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Using the long-lived asset impairment model under ASC 360

choices.label: B
choices.text: Using the goodwill impairment model under ASC 350

choices.label: C
choices.text: ROU assets are not subject to impairment testing

choices.label: D
choices.text: Using the indefinite-lived intangible asset model under ASC 350

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-9, the ROU asset is subject to impairment testing in accordance with ASC 360-10 (impairment of long-lived assets). The lessee tests for impairment when events or circumstances indicate that the carrying amount may not be recoverable. The recoverability test compares the carrying amount to the sum of undiscounted future cash flows. If not recoverable, the impairment loss is the excess of carrying amount over fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-35-9により、使用権資産はASC 360-10（長期性資産の減損）に従い減損テストを行う。帳簿価額が回収不能の可能性がある事象が発生した場合にテスト。回収可能性テストは帳簿価額と割引前キャッシュフローを比較。

questions.stem: Lessee H guarantees a residual value of $40,000 to the lessor. At lease commencement, the expected residual value is $25,000. What amount of the residual value guarantee is included in the lessee's lease payments for measurement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5(d), the lessee includes in lease payments amounts probable of being owed under a residual value guarantee. The expected deficiency = guaranteed amount ($40,000) − expected residual value ($25,000) = $15,000. This $15,000 is included in the lease liability measurement. If the expected residual value equaled or exceeded the guarantee, no amount would be included.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5(d)により、残存価値保証の下で支払が見込まれる金額をリース料に含める。予想不足額 = 保証額$40,000 − 予想残存価値$25,000 = $15,000。この$15,000をリース負債の測定に含める。

questions.stem: Seller-Lessee Corp. sells a building (carrying value $600,000, fair value $1,000,000) for $1,000,000 and leases it back under a 3-year operating lease (building has 20 years remaining useful life). The present value of lease payments is $150,000. How much gain is recognized at the transaction date?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $0 — the gain is deferred

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-40-25-4, when a sale-leaseback qualifies as a sale, the seller-lessee recognizes only the gain that relates to the rights transferred to the buyer-lessor. Gain = (Fair value − Carrying value) × (1 − PV of leaseback payments / Fair value). Total gain = $1,000,000 − $600,000 = $400,000. Retained portion = $150,000 / $1,000,000 = 15%. Gain recognized = $400,000 × (1 − 0.15) = $400,000 × 0.85 = $340,000. The remaining $60,000 is an adjustment to the ROU asset.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-40-25-4により、売却リースバックが売却要件を満たす場合、買手・貸手に移転した権利に関する利益のみ認識。利益 = $400,000 × (1 − $150,000/$1,000,000) = $400,000 × 85% = $340,000。残り$60,000は使用権資産の調整。

questions.stem: Company J enters a 10-month lease with no renewal option. The lease has a purchase option that is not reasonably certain to be exercised. Can Company J elect the short-term lease exemption?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because the lease term is 12 months or less and there is no purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the lease has a purchase option

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only if the lease payment is below a materiality threshold

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, the short-term exemption only applies to month-to-month leases

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-25-2, a short-term lease is a lease that, at commencement, has a lease term of 12 months or less AND does not include a purchase option that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise. This 10-month lease meets both criteria (term ≤ 12 months, purchase option not reasonably certain). The lessee may elect to recognize lease payments on a straight-line basis without recording a ROU asset or lease liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-2により、短期リースとは開始時にリース期間が12ヶ月以下で、行使がほぼ確実な購入オプションを含まないリース。10ヶ月で購入オプション行使が確実でないため、両条件を満たし短期リース免除を選択可能。

questions.subtopic: Prepaid Rent Effect on ROU Asset
questions.stem: Lessee K enters a 5-year operating lease and makes a prepayment of $20,000 at lease commencement. The present value of remaining lease payments (excluding the prepayment) is $300,000. What is the initial ROU asset?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, the initial ROU asset is measured as the lease liability ($300,000) plus any lease payments made at or before commencement ($20,000) minus any lease incentives received, plus any initial direct costs. With no incentives or initial direct costs: ROU asset = $300,000 + $20,000 = $320,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5により、使用権資産 = リース負債($300,000) + 開始時の前払リース料($20,000) − リースインセンティブ + 初期直接コスト。インセンティブと初期直接コストがないため、$300,000 + $20,000 = $320,000。

questions.stem: Entity L contracts for the use of a specific piece of equipment. The supplier has a substantive right to substitute the equipment with an alternative asset at any time during the contract period. Does this arrangement contain a lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because the equipment is specified in the contract

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the supplier has a substantive substitution right, so there is no identified asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, if the customer controls the equipment during use

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because substitution rights always negate a lease

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-10, an asset is not identified if the supplier has a substantive right to substitute the asset throughout the period of use. A substitution right is substantive if the supplier has the practical ability to substitute alternative assets AND would benefit economically from the substitution. Since the supplier has a substantive substitution right, there is no identified asset and therefore no lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-10により、供給者が使用期間を通じて実質的な代替権を有する場合、資産は識別されない。代替権が実質的とは、供給者が代替可能な能力を持ち代替から経済的便益を得る場合。実質的代替権があるため、リースに該当しない。

questions.stem: Lessor M classifies a lease as an operating lease. The underlying asset has a cost of $1,200,000, an estimated useful life of 15 years, and an estimated residual value of $120,000. The lease term is 5 years. How should Lessor M depreciate the asset during the lease term?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Depreciate over 5 years (the lease term)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Depreciate over 15 years (the useful life) on a straight-line basis

choices.label: C
choices.text: No depreciation during the lease term

choices.label: D
choices.text: Depreciate over 15 years using an accelerated method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-11, in an operating lease, the lessor retains the underlying asset on its balance sheet and depreciates it in accordance with ASC 360 (property, plant, and equipment). The asset is depreciated over its useful life (15 years), not the lease term, using a method consistent with the lessor's normal depreciation policy (generally straight-line). Annual depreciation = ($1,200,000 − $120,000) / 15 = $72,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-11により、オペレーティングリースでは貸手が資産を保有し、ASC 360に従い耐用年数（15年）で減価償却する。リース期間（5年）ではない。年間減価償却 = ($1,200,000 − $120,000) ÷ 15 = $72,000。

questions.stem: Lessee-Sublessor N has a 10-year head lease for office space classified as an operating lease. N subleases the space to a third party for 7 years. When classifying the sublease, what asset does Sublessor N use as the reference?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The right-of-use asset arising from the head lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: The underlying asset (the office space)

choices.label: C
choices.text: The remaining lease payments under the head lease

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fair value of a new direct lease of similar space

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-2(b), an intermediate lessor (sublessor) classifies a sublease by reference to the underlying right-of-use asset arising from the head lease, not the underlying asset itself. This means the sublease classification is based on the ROU asset's remaining term and other factors. Since the sublease term (7 years) relative to the ROU asset's remaining term (10 years) is 70%, additional analysis is needed to determine if it meets the 'major part' threshold.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-2(b)により、中間貸手（転貸人）はヘッドリースから生じる使用権資産を基準にサブリースを分類する。原資産そのものではない。サブリース期間（7年）を使用権資産の残存期間（10年）との関係で分類判定する。
